title: dacryodes elmeri
intro: Dacryodes elmeri, also known as the Philippine palm tree or Palosapi, is a species of plant in the family Burseraceae, native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Dacryodes elmeri include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic to clean wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be applied topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
